articles.headline: JPMorgan blocks Hong Kong staff from accessing Anthropic models
articles.summary: JPMorgan Chase has reportedly stopped staff in Hong Kong from using Anthropic’s AI models, reflecting tighter scrutiny around cross-border AI use. This matters to businesses because it highlights compliance, data-governance, and jurisdictional risks that can affect enterprise AI deployments.

articles.headline: Waymo recalls 3,871 robotaxis after software issue raises safety concerns
articles.summary: Waymo is recalling thousands of robotaxis after a software problem could cause vehicles to continue driving in a closed freeway construction zone. The incident is relevant for businesses because it underscores the operational and legal risk of deploying autonomous systems before edge cases are fully controlled.

articles.headline: China’s humanoid robot boom raises price-war concerns
articles.summary: A surge of new entrants in China’s humanoid robotics market is fueling fears of an overcrowded sector and aggressive price competition. For businesses, this could accelerate cost declines for robots and automation tools, but it also signals heightened supplier risk and possible instability among vendors.
